easy.title: Verify Your Python Installation
easy.description:
Write a Python script that prints your Python version information without using any import statement or any command-line flags. The script should output two things on separate lines:
1. The text 'Python is installed and working!'
2. The text 'Ready to learn Python!'
easy.starterCode:
# Your task:
# Print two lines as described above
# This is your first Python program!
easy.expectedOutput:
Python is installed and working!
Ready to learn Python!
easy.solution:
print('Python is installed and working!')
print('Ready to learn Python!')
hints: The print() function outputs text to the screen
Call print() once for each line you want to output
Text strings in Python are wrapped in single quotes '' or double quotes ""

medium.title: System Information Reporter
medium.description:
Write a Python script that prints a formatted report about the Python environment. Your script must:
1. Import the sys module
2. Print the Python major version number (e.g., 3)
3. Print the Python minor version number (e.g., 12)
4. Print the full path to the Python executable
5. Print how many directories Python searches for modules (the length of sys.path)

Format each line clearly (e.g., 'Major version: 3').
medium.starterCode:
import sys

# Access sys.version_info for major and minor version
# Access sys.executable for the interpreter path
# Access sys.path for the module search path list
medium.expectedOutput:
Major version: 3
Minor version: 12
Executable: /usr/local/bin/python3
Module search directories: 8
medium.solution:
import sys

print(f'Major version: {sys.version_info.major}')
print(f'Minor version: {sys.version_info.minor}')
print(f'Executable: {sys.executable}')
print(f'Module search directories: {len(sys.path)}')
hints: sys.version_info is a named tuple with .major, .minor, .micro attributes
sys.executable is a string containing the path
sys.path is a list — use len() to count its elements
Use f-strings to embed variables in strings: f'text {variable}'

hard.title: Python Environment Inspector
hard.description:
Write a script that checks whether the Python environment meets a set of minimum requirements and prints a summary report. Your script must:
1. Check that Python version is 3.8 or higher — if not, print a warning
2. Check that pip is importable (import pip) — if not, print a warning
3. Try to import a common standard library module (e.g., 'json') and confirm it's available
4. Print a final verdict: 'Environment OK' if all checks passed, or 'Environment has issues' if any failed

All checks should complete even if one fails — do not let an exception stop the script.
hard.starterCode:
import sys

all_ok = True

# Check 1: Python version
# sys.version_info.major and sys.version_info.minor

# Check 2: pip importable
# Use try/except ImportError

# Check 3: json importable
# Use try/except ImportError

# Print verdict
hard.expectedOutput:
✓ Python 3.12 — meets minimum requirement (3.8+)
✓ pip is available
✓ Standard library (json) is available
Environment OK
hard.solution:
import sys

all_ok = True

# Check 1: Python version
major = sys.version_info.major
minor = sys.version_info.minor
if major >= 3 and minor >= 8:
    print(f'✓ Python {major}.{minor} — meets minimum requirement (3.8+)')
else:
    print(f'✗ Python {major}.{minor} — upgrade required (need 3.8+)')
    all_ok = False

# Check 2: pip
try:
    import pip
    print('✓ pip is available')
except ImportError:
    print('✗ pip not found — run: python3 -m ensurepip --upgrade')
    all_ok = False

# Check 3: Standard library
try:
    import json
    print('✓ Standard library (json) is available')
except ImportError:
    print('✗ Standard library broken — reinstall Python')
    all_ok = False

if all_ok:
    print('Environment OK')
else:
    print('Environment has issues')
hints: Use sys.version_info.major >= 3 and sys.version_info.minor >= 8 for the version check
Wrap each import in try/except ImportError to handle failures gracefully
Use a boolean flag (all_ok) that starts as True and gets set to False when a check fails
The script should print the verdict at the end regardless of individual check results

debugging.title: Fix the Installation Checker
debugging.description: The following script has 3 bugs. Find and fix them all.
debugging.buggyCode:
import sys

# Bug 1: Wrong attribute name
version = sys.version_info.Version
print(f'Python {version}')

# Bug 2: Wrong comparison
if sys.version_info.major = 3:
    print('Running Python 3')

# Bug 3: Missing the module search path variable
print(f'Search paths: {len(system.path)}')
debugging.fixedCode:
import sys

# Fix 1: Correct attribute is .major (lowercase), not .Version
version = sys.version_info.major
print(f'Python {version}')

# Fix 2: == for comparison, not = (= is assignment)
if sys.version_info.major == 3:
    print('Running Python 3')

# Fix 3: sys.path not system.path
print(f'Search paths: {len(sys.path)}')
bugs: sys.version_info.Version should be sys.version_info.major — Python attributes are lowercase; Version is not a valid attribute
if sys.version_info.major = 3 uses = (assignment) instead of == (equality comparison). This is a SyntaxError in Python.
system.path should be sys.path — 'system' is not imported; only 'sys' is
